title: cystopteris fragilis subsp. kansuana
intro: Cystopteris fragilis subsp. kansuana, also known as the Kansu ciliate fern, is a subspecies of the fragile fern plant (Cystopteris fragilis) native to Asia. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Tibetan healing practices, and is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Cystopteris fragilis subsp. kansuana include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea infusions or dried as a powder to be consumed in capsules or added to other remedies.
